# Golarion — Factions & Organizations Reference

Structured reference for the AI GM's NPC generation, quest hooks, and political context.
Covers Inner Sea and Tian Xia major organizations.

---

## Adventuring & Scholarly Organizations

### Pathfinder Society
- **Type:** Adventuring and exploration guild
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral (members vary widely)
- **Headquarters:** Grand Lodge, Absalom
- **Leader:** Venture-Captain system under the Decemvirate (10 masked leaders)
- **Reach:** Global — lodges in most major cities on both continents
- **Goals:** Explore, report, cooperate. Document discoveries, recover artifacts, map the unknown.
- **Methods:** Send agents (Pathfinders) on missions. Publish findings in the Pathfinder Chronicles. Maintain an extensive library and artifact collection.
- **Allies:** Generally welcomed by scholarly institutions
- **Enemies:** Aspis Consortium (rival), nations that distrust foreign agents
- **Ranks:** Field agent → Venture-Captain → Decemvirate
- **Campaign Relevance:** The default adventuring patron. PCs can be Pathfinders sent on missions. Lodges provide safe houses, quest hooks, and information. The Decemvirate's secrecy creates internal intrigue.
- **NPC Personality Notes:** Venture-Captains range from enthusiastic scholars to jaded bureaucrats. Field agents tend to be resourceful, curious, and occasionally reckless.

### Aspis Consortium
- **Type:** Mercantile/criminal exploration company
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Evil
- **Headquarters:** Ostenso, Cheliax (officially); operates globally
- **Leader:** Board of anonymous Patrons
- **Reach:** Global trade network with agents in most cities
- **Goals:** Acquire wealth and powerful artifacts by any means. Undercut competitors. Monopolize trade routes.
- **Methods:** Hire adventurers for artifact recovery, bribe officials, sabotage rivals, smuggle contraband. Professional and well-funded but amoral.
- **Allies:** Wealthy clients, corrupt officials
- **Enemies:** Pathfinder Society (primary rival), law enforcement in good-aligned nations
- **Campaign Relevance:** Excellent recurring villain organization. Well-funded, professional, and everywhere. Their agents are competent and dangerous. Competing with Aspis agents for the same artifact is a classic PF2e scenario.

---

## Military & Knightly Orders

### Knights of Lastwall
- **Type:** Crusader military order
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Good
- **Headquarters:** Absalom (exiled from destroyed Lastwall)
- **Leader:** Various knight-commanders (decentralized since Lastwall fell)
- **Reach:** Absalom, scattered chapterhouses, mobile warbands near the Gravelands
- **Goals:** Destroy the Whispering Tyrant (Tar-Baphon), reclaim Lastwall, protect the living from undead
- **Methods:** Military campaigns against undead, intelligence gathering on Tar-Baphon's forces, training new crusaders, divine magic against undead
- **Allies:** Iomedae's church, Pharasma's church, Ustalav (reluctantly)
- **Enemies:** Tar-Baphon, the Whispering Way, Gravelands undead armies
- **Campaign Relevance:** Displaced crusaders with a desperate mission. Excellent patron for lawful good campaigns. They're under-resourced and fighting an overwhelming enemy — high stakes, low safety net.

### Hellknights
- **Type:** Lawful militant orders
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Neutral (officially; varies by order)
- **Headquarters:** Citadels across Cheliax, Isger, and beyond
- **Leader:** Each order has its own Lictor (supreme commander)
- **Reach:** Primarily Cheliax and neighboring nations; some presence in Absalom and Varisia
- **Goals:** Impose absolute law and order. Eradicate chaos. The law exists to protect civilization from its own worst impulses.
- **Methods:** Military force, legal authority, intimidation. Train with devil-summoning to understand and resist chaos. Brutal but principled.
- **Major Orders:**
  - **Order of the Rack** — Suppress dangerous knowledge, censor forbidden texts
  - **Order of the Nail** — Tame frontiers, destroy "barbarism"
  - **Order of the Scourge** — Root out organized crime and corruption
  - **Order of the Gate** — Summon and bind devils (for study and use)
  - **Order of the Pyre** — Destroy false cults and dangerous religions
  - **Order of the Chain** — Capture fugitives, enforce justice
  - **Order of the Godclaw** — Worship a heretical combined pantheon of five lawful deities
- **Allies:** Chelish government, local law enforcement (when convenient)
- **Enemies:** Criminal organizations, chaotic factions, freedom movements
- **Campaign Relevance:** Morally complex antagonists or allies. They're not always wrong — sometimes the law really does need enforcing — but their methods are brutal. Individual Hellknights range from principled to tyrannical. The Order of the Godclaw is heretical to all five deities they worship, creating interesting religious tension.

### Eagle Knights (Andoran)
- **Type:** National military and special forces
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Good / Chaotic Good
- **Headquarters:** Almas, Andoran
- **Leader:** Supreme Elect of Andoran
- **Reach:** Andoran, the Inner Sea (naval operations), covert operations globally
- **Goals:** Protect Andoran's democracy, abolish slavery, counter tyranny
- **Branches:**
  - **Golden Legion** — Andoran's standing army
  - **Steel Falcons** — Covert agents working to undermine slavery and tyranny abroad
  - **Gray Corsairs** — Anti-slavery naval fleet that intercepts slave ships
- **Allies:** Andoran citizenry, Bellflower Network, free peoples everywhere
- **Enemies:** Cheliax, Katapesh slavers, any slave-trading nation
- **Campaign Relevance:** Freedom fighters and abolitionists. Steel Falcon missions are excellent espionage/stealth adventures. Gray Corsair campaigns are naval action. Good-aligned faction with genuine tension — their covert operations sometimes cause diplomatic incidents.

---

## Criminal & Underground Organizations

### Bellflower Network
- **Type:** Underground railroad for slaves
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Good
- **Headquarters:** Decentralized cells
- **Reach:** Cheliax, Katapesh, Absalom, anywhere slavery exists
- **Goals:** Free slaves and smuggle them to safety. Destroy the institution of slavery.
- **Methods:** Safe houses, coded messages, disguises, bribery, tunnels. Non-violent when possible, but will fight when cornered.
- **Allies:** Eagle Knights (Gray Corsairs and Steel Falcons), Andoran, Cayden Cailean's church
- **Enemies:** Slave traders, Chelish government, Katapesh's criminal elements
- **Campaign Relevance:** The moral underground. Bellflower tillers (agents) make excellent allies or contacts. Smuggling slaves to freedom creates tense, high-stakes social and stealth encounters.

### Sczarni
- **Type:** Varisian organized crime families
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral to Neutral Evil
- **Headquarters:** Varisia (Magnimar, Riddleport, Korvosa underworld)
- **Reach:** Varisia primarily; Varisian diaspora communities elsewhere
- **Goals:** Profit through crime while maintaining Varisian cultural identity
- **Methods:** Smuggling, protection rackets, gambling, fencing stolen goods, confidence schemes. Organized in family units. Strong code of loyalty within the family.
- **Campaign Relevance:** Local organized crime with cultural depth. Not cartoonishly evil — they're families protecting their own in a world that marginalizes Varisians. Individual Sczarni range from lovable rogues to ruthless criminals.

### Night Heralds
- **Type:** Doomsday cult
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Evil
- **Headquarters:** Unknown (cells scattered)
- **Reach:** Sporadic presence across the Inner Sea
- **Goals:** Bring about the end of the world. Believe Aroden's death was a sign that existence should end.
- **Methods:** Summoning entities from the Dark Tapestry (Lovecraftian space horrors), acquiring weapons of mass destruction, infiltrating positions of power
- **Campaign Relevance:** Apocalyptic villains with Lovecraftian flair. Good long-arc antagonists whose cells the party uncovers piece by piece.

### Whispering Way
- **Type:** Undead cult / necromantic secret society
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Evil
- **Headquarters:** Mobile; historical ties to Ustalav
- **Reach:** Wherever undead power exists — Ustalav, Gravelands, Geb
- **Goals:** Achieve immortality through undeath, serve the Whispering Tyrant, spread necromantic knowledge
- **Methods:** Necromancy, political infiltration, ancient rituals, manipulating undead armies. Members communicate through whispered code phrases (never writing their secrets down).
- **Allies:** Tar-Baphon (the Whispering Tyrant), Urgathoa cultists, free-willed undead
- **Enemies:** Knights of Lastwall, Pharasma's church, all living civilizations
- **Campaign Relevance:** Undead-themed recurring villains. Their "no written records" tradition means the party must capture members alive for information. Deeply tied to Ustalav horror campaigns and the Gravelands.

---

## Religious Organizations

### Magaambya
- **Type:** Oldest magical academy on Golarion
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Good
- **Headquarters:** Nantambu, Mwangi Expanse
- **Leader:** Council of Ten Magic Warriors
- **Reach:** Mwangi Expanse; respected globally
- **Goals:** Preserve and advance magical knowledge. Protect the Mwangi Expanse. Maintain Old-Mage Jatembe's legacy.
- **Methods:** Education, research, diplomacy, magical intervention when necessary
- **Campaign Relevance:** The premier magical academy — think Hogwarts for Golarion but older and more serious. Graduated Magaambyan scholars are respected everywhere. Good patron for wizard/caster campaigns.

### Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye
- **Type:** Secret occult society
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral
- **Headquarters:** Ustalav
- **Goals:** Accumulate forbidden knowledge, protect Ustalav from supernatural threats, understand the nature of reality
- **Methods:** Secret lodges, coded communications, occult research, political influence
- **Campaign Relevance:** Ustalavic secret society that dabbles in dangerous knowledge "for the greater good." Morally ambiguous — they fight genuine threats but their methods are questionable. Good patrons for occult/horror campaigns.

---

## Political & Trade Organizations

### Firebrands
- **Type:** Disorganized revolutionary movement
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Good / Chaotic Neutral
- **Headquarters:** No central base (that would be too organized)
- **Reach:** Global — anywhere there's tyranny to oppose
- **Goals:** Oppose tyranny, seek thrills, make a name for yourself, look good doing it
- **Methods:** Dramatic direct action, public spectacles, daring rescues, humiliating the powerful. Style matters as much as substance.
- **Campaign Relevance:** Swashbuckling freedom fighters who prize flair. Think Robin Hood meets Zorro. Excellent patron for charismatic, action-oriented campaigns. Their lack of organization is both strength (can't be destroyed by cutting off the head) and weakness (poor coordination).

### Envoy's Alliance
- **Type:** Diplomatic corps
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral
- **Headquarters:** Absalom
- **Goals:** Resolve conflicts through negotiation, maintain peace, establish trade agreements
- **Methods:** Diplomacy, mediation, intelligence gathering, economic leverage
- **Campaign Relevance:** Social encounter specialists. Good patron for campaigns focused on negotiation, political intrigue, and preventing wars rather than fighting them.

### Freehold of Goka Trade Council
- **Type:** Merchant alliance
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral
- **Headquarters:** Goka, Tian Xia
- **Reach:** All of Tian Xia's major trade routes, some Inner Sea connections
- **Goals:** Maintain Goka's trade dominance, ensure free movement of goods, resolve trade disputes
- **Campaign Relevance:** The economic backbone of Tian Xia trade. Controlling access to Goka means controlling eastern commerce. Good patron for trade-route and caravan-based adventures.

---

## Faction Relationships Quick Reference

### Allied Factions
- Pathfinder Society ↔ Magaambya (scholarly respect)
- Eagle Knights ↔ Bellflower Network (anti-slavery)
- Knights of Lastwall ↔ Iomedae church ↔ Pharasma church (anti-undead)
- Firebrands ↔ Bellflower Network (anti-tyranny)

### Rival/Enemy Factions
- Pathfinder Society vs. Aspis Consortium (exploration rivalry)
- Eagle Knights vs. Cheliax/Hellknights (freedom vs. authoritarian law)
- Knights of Lastwall vs. Whispering Way (crusaders vs. undead cultists)
- Firebrands vs. Hellknights (chaos vs. law)

### Complex Relationships
- Hellknights ↔ Cheliax — Hellknights serve law, not Cheliax's diabolism. Alignment of convenience, not ideology.
- Pathfinder Society ↔ Hellknights — Some cooperation on matter of mutual interest, mutual distrust otherwise.
- Sczarni ↔ Local authorities — Tolerated in some cities, hunted in others.
